name: Orange Beef with Sun-Dried Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Orange zest, finely julienned (from 1 large orange)
2 tablespoons Peeled Ginger root, finely julienned
1 Jalapeno pepper, preferably red, seeds removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the flank steak in half lengthwise and then into 1/8-inch diagonal slices against the grain.
2. Mix brandy and soy sauce in a bowl. Add steak; mix. Stir in cornstarch, then oil, coating the meat well. Let marinate at room temperature for 20 minutes to one hour.
3. Cook orange zest: Bring water and sugar to boil over high heat. Add zest and boil for 5 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, squeeze dry, and set aside.
4. Soak sun-dried tomatoes in warm water for about 10 minutes until softened. Slice julienne-style.
5. Heat oil in a large skillet or wok until very hot (almost smoking). Add steak pieces, separating them as they cook. Sear golden brown for about 2 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
6. Reduce heat to low, leaving only 2 tablespoons of oil. Add orange zest, sun-dried tomatoes, scallions, garlic, ginger, and jalapeno. Cook until garlic turns golden.
7. Return steak to skillet, add stock, stir well, and cook for 3 minutes until liquid evaporates completely. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Serve hot over rice or in sandwiches with good bread.
This recipe serves 2 as a main dish or 4 as part of a sandwich.
